commit.message:
Fix Android Auto truncating podcast episode lists (#54)

* Initial plan

* Fix Android Auto not showing all podcast episodes

Remove the artificial 100-episode per-page cap in onLoadChildren and
the "More episodes…" FLAG_BROWSABLE item that were preventing Android
Auto from seeing the full episode list.

MediaBrowserServiceCompat.applyOptions() slices onLoadChildren results
by EXTRA_PAGE / EXTRA_PAGE_SIZE before sending them to the client.
The old approach capped results at 100 and added a "More episodes…"
item that was silently dropped by this framework slicing, making
episodes beyond page 1 inaccessible.

The fix returns ALL episodes as a flat list. The framework's applyOptions
then correctly pages through the complete list as Android Auto requests
each page. fetchEpisodesPaged(0, Int.MAX_VALUE) adds no extra network or
memory cost since the RSS feed is always fully downloaded before slicing.

Backward compatibility: the podcast ID extraction still strips any
legacy ":start=<n>:count=<m>" suffix so cached navigation items from the
old approach do not crash.

fix: four search quality issues identified from real output audit

1. Fix hyphen stripping in returned content (search-utils.ts, graph-context-serializer.ts)
   - stripInlineTags() used /(\s*-\s*)+/g which matched bare hyphens with zero
     surrounding whitespace, stripping EVERY hyphen in content (p-6→p 6, text-xl→text xl)
   - Fixed to /[ \t]+-[ \t]+/g: only strips space-hyphen-space separators (YAML list markers)
   - Database content was always correct; bug was at search result serialization time

2. Newline-aware chunk splitter (atomizer-service.ts)
   - Max-size enforcer used binary search to find a byte boundary then split there
   - This cut mid-word and mid-identifier (e.g. 'fr' + 'equency')
   - After binary search, walk back to nearest '\n' before the split point
   - Falls back to byte boundary only if no newline exists in the chunk

3. Test fixture tag filtering (atomizer-service.ts TAG_BLACKLIST_PATTERNS)
   - Unit test mock atoms with tags like #Tag, #Tag1, #sharedA, #sharedB, #Word
     were being extracted as real tags when the github repo was ingested
   - Added patterns: /^#tag\d*$/i, /^#shared[a-z]$/i, /^#word\d*$/i, /^#fixture/i,
     /^#mock/i, /^#dummy/i, /^#sample[a-z0-9]*$/i

4. camelCase query expansion (query-parser.ts, search.ts)
   - FTS 'simple' config treats findAnchors as one opaque token 'findanchors'
   - Added expandCamelCase(): splits on camelCase/PascalCase boundaries
   - findAnchors → [findanchors, find, anchors]
   - PhysicsTagWalker → [physicstagwalker, physics, tag, walker]
   - tsTerms in findAnchors() now expanded before building to_tsquery string

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add FlakyClassification DU that distinguishes environmental flakiness
(timing, race conditions) from FsCheck property counterexamples (real
bugs found via random testing). This prevents false-positive flaky
labels on property-based tests.

- FlakyClassification: Insufficient | Stable | Environmental | PropertyCounterexample
- isFsCheckFailure: regex extraction of counterexample from FsCheck messages
- classifyFlakiness: combines flip history with failure message analysis
- TestRunExplanation.IsFlaky replaced with FlakyClassification field
- MCP serialization backward-compatible (keeps IsFlaky bool + adds classification)
- 13 new tests (6 isFsCheckFailure + 7 classifyFlakiness)
- 10 existing TestRunExplainer tests updated and passing

* refactor: remove quarkus-hibernate-validator; use programmatic validation only

All validation is now done in FluencyClient.validateConfig() which throws
IllegalStateException with clear messages at startup. Remove:
- quarkus-hibernate-validator from runtime/pom.xml
- quarkus-hibernate-validator-deployment from deployment/pom.xml
- Jakarta Bean Validation annotations (@NotBlank, @Min, @Max, @Positive)
  from FluencyConfig

Co-authored-by: yuokada <387284+yuokada@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: merge config validation tests into QuarkusFluencyFluentdTest.java

Consolidate FluencyConfigValidationTest, FluencyConfigPortValidationTest,
FluencyConfigBufferSizeValidationTest and TestUtils into a single file
QuarkusFluencyFluentdTest.java using multiple package-private top-level
classes. Each class retains its own @RegisterExtension so the separate
Quarkus application contexts are preserved.

QuarkusDevModeTest requires a public class for reflection-based proxy
creation, so QuarkusFluencyFluentdDevModeTest stays in its own file.

The hasCause helper is inlined as a package-private static method on
FluencyConfigValidationTest, removing the need for TestUtils.java.
